pending.body: Wire planned-elsewhere findings through the actual final-review/reconciliation path instead of only adding a standalone helper. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:137-154` still only allows free-form amendments, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1523-1553` still marks any accepted amendment as `restart_required`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7359-7378` still turns every accepted final-review item into a `QueuedAmendment`. The new handler in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:81-158` is not invoked from that flow, so there is currently no way to carry `mapped_to_bead_id` through review or let the active bead complete without a reopen/restart loop.

Make `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` transactional across `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` and the milestone journal. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2352-2380` the mapping file is appended first and the `PlannedElsewhereMapped` journal event is appended second, with no rollback if `append_event` fails. A journal write error will therefore leave a persisted mapping with no audit event.

Tighten stale-bead fallback so only definitive missing/closed bead results are downgraded to warnings. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:55-73`, any `br show` failure is treated as `bead_verified = false` and reconciliation proceeds. Timeouts, parse failures, or repo/configuration errors would therefore be misclassified as stale-bead cases instead of surfacing an operator-visible failure.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
